---
title: "Rapport d’analyse statistique"
subtitle: "Titre Court : Titre Long"
author: "Mathilde Boissel"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  word_document:
  toc: yes
toc_depth: 3
always_allow_html: true
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---
  
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
root_directory <- "C:/Users/boissel_mathilde/Documents" 
setwd(root_directory)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  results = "asis",
  #root_directory = root_directory,
  size = "small",
  include = TRUE,
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dpi = 150,
  tidy = FALSE,
  crop = TRUE,
  autodep = TRUE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.pos = "!H",
  cache = FALSE,
  cache.path = paste0(root_directory, "/cache/"),
  fig.path = paste0(root_directory, "/cache/")
  # fig.width = unit(12, "cm"),
  # fig.height = unit(15, "cm")
)


#### lib #### 

library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(R2Excel)
# packageVersion("R2Excel") # v ... # to check
library(readxl)
# viz
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(patchwork)

# ...

#### path ####

# project_directory <- file.path(
#   "Volume:", "EtudeClinique","XXX",
#   "Biostatistiques", "Analyse"
# )
# output_directory <- file.path(
#   project_directory, "Output"
# )

#### Load objects and format ####
# setwd(output_directory)

# annexe_directory <- file.path(
#   project_directory, "Report", "report_annexes"
# )
# dir.create(annexe_directory, showWarnings = FALSE)


#### fct ####

# source(file.path(project_directory, "Script", "get_detail_LMM.R"))
# source(file.path(project_directory, "Script", "get_detail_GLMM.R"))

#### data ####

# dt <- readRDS(file.path(
#   project_directory,
#   "Data", 
#   "formated",
#   "XXX.rds"
# ))
# 
# dt2 <- readRDS(
#   file.path(output_directory, "01-XXX.rds")
# )

```

<br> 
  
|                           |                       |
|---------------------------|-----------------------|
| Investigateur(s)          | xxx                   |
| Centre(s)/service(s)      | xxx                   |
| Nombre total de patients  | xxx                   |
| Date de réalisation de l’analyse          | XX/XX/2025  |
| Biostatisticien en charge de l’analyse    | Mathilde BOISSEL|
| Contact                   | boissel.mathilde@ghicl.net      |

  
<br> 
  
\newpage

# Table des matières

----
  
**TOC --here to update in word menu** 

<!-- TOC to update in word -->

<br> 

\newpage 

# Design de l’étude
  
----

**blabla --here**


# Objectifs et critères de jugement 

----

## a)	Objectifs

_Objectif principal_ : 

**blabla --here**

_Objectifs secondaires_ : 

1) **blabla --here**

2) **blabla --here**


<br>

## b)	Critères de jugement

_Critère de jugement principal_ : 

**blabla --here**

_Critères de jugement secondaires_ : 

1) **blabla --here**

2) **blabla --here**

<br>

# Population d’analyse

----

**blabla --here**

# Modalités d’administration du traitement de l’étude (si applicable)

----

**blabla --here**

# Traitements autorisés / interdits (si applicable)

----

**blabla --here**

# Plan d’analyse statistique (en français)

----

**blabla --here**

# Flowchart

----			

**blabla --here**

<!-- Fig 1 : Rythme des visites -->

<!-- ![](..\outputs\02-gg_visites_date.png) -->


```{r fig}
# cat("Fig 1 : xxx.")
# fig1 <- readRDS(file.path(output_directory, "03-fig1.rds"))
# print(fig1)
# cat("\n\n")
# cat("<br>")

## make the copy of figs...
# example: 
# copydone <- invisible(
#   file.copy(
#     from = list.files(
#       path = output_directory, pattern = "^03-fig_(.*)png$", full.names = TRUE
#     ),
#     to = annexe_directory, 
#     overwrite = TRUE
#   )
# )
```


# Modifications de la base de données

----			

Les modifications suivantes ont été effectuées sur la base de données : 

|  ID patient  | Variable |  Ancienne valeur  | Nouvelle valeur |
|--------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
|              |          |                   |          |
|              |          |                   |          |


Les variables suivantes ont été créées : 
-	Var1 : somme des sous-scores du score X par exemple


# Déviations

----			

Inclus à tort, compliance au protocole, recours aux traitements interdits, etc


# Analyse descriptive

----			

## a)	Description de la population

Tab 1 : Caractéristiques de la population – variables qualitatives

```{r tab1}
my_title <- "Table 1 : ..."
tab <- readxl::read_excel(file.path(output_directory, "02-desc_pop.xlsx"), sheet = 1) 
gt::gt(tab) %>% 
  tab_header(title = my_title) %>%
  opt_row_striping()
```

Tab 2 : Caractéristiques de la population – variable quantitative

```{r tab2}
my_title <- "Table 2 : ..."
tab <- readxl::read_excel(file.path(output_directory, "02-desc_pop.xlsx"), sheet = 2) 
gt::gt(tab) %>% 
  tab_header(title = my_title) %>%
  opt_row_striping()
```

Commentaires : 

+ **blabla --here**

+ **CI, CNI --here**

## b)	Description des critères de jugement

**blabla --here**

```{r tab3}
tab3 <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  setDT(readxl::read_excel(
      path = file.path(
        output_directory, "02-desc_by_group.xlsx"
      ),
      col_names = TRUE
    ))
))
tab3$Valeurs_manquantes  <- NULL
```


```{r tab3_show}
my_title <- "Table 3 : Description by group"
tab_ex <- tab3

gt_table <- gt(tab_ex) %>%
  tab_header(title = my_title) %>% 
  text_transform(
    locations = cells_body(columns = c("Label", "Variable", "Modalites", "Nb_mesures")),
    fn = function(x) {
      ifelse(is.na(x) | x %in% "NA", "", x)
    }
  ) %>% 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "cadetblue3"),
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = Variable 
    )
  ) %>% 
  opt_row_striping()
gt_table
```


## c)	Description des visites

**blabla --here**

```{r tab4}
tab4 <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  setDT(readxl::read_excel(
      path = file.path(output_directory, "02-count_by_ALLERGENE.xlsx"),
      col_names = TRUE
    ))
))
setcolorder(
  x = tab4, # put category and label in first cols
  neworder = unique(c("ALLERGENE_cat", "ALLERGENE_label", names(tab4)))
)
tab4 <- data.table::dcast(
  tab4,
  ALLERGENE_cat + ALLERGENE_label + ALLERGENE ~ VISITE, 
  value.var = "nb_patients"
)
```

```{r tab4_show}
tab_ex <- tab4
my_title <- paste0(
  "Table 3 : Comptage inclusion / Allergène / Visite"
)

# gt style ##
gt_table <- gt(tab_ex, groupname_col = "ALLERGENE_cat") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = my_title
  ) %>% 
  text_transform(
    locations = cells_body(columns = starts_with("V")),
    fn = function(x) {
      ifelse(is.na(x) | x %in% "NA", "", x)
    }
  ) %>% 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "nb_patients / Visites",
    columns = starts_with("V")
  ) %>% 
  opt_row_striping()

gt_table
```


## d)	Description des traitements

**blabla --here**

# Analyse du critère principal

----			

**blabla --here**

**exemple**

```{r tab11a}
# exemple
tab11a <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  setDT(readxl::read_excel(
    path = file.path(
      output_directory, "03-res_Cox_ageincl_by_allergene.xlsx"
    ),
    col_names = TRUE, sheet = "event_Desen"
  ))
))

## check : 
# paste0(unique(tab11a$warnings), collapse = ", ")

tab11a <- tab11a[is.na(warnings), ][, warnings := NULL]
tab11a$wald.test <- NULL
tab11a$p.val_Schoenfeld <- NULL
tab11a$p.val_Spline <- NULL

```

```{r tab11a_show}

my_title <- "Table 11A : Lien évènement Désensibilisation ~ AGE à l'inclusion / allergène"

tab_ex <- tab11a
gt_table <- gt(tab_ex[order(Allergenes), ]) %>%
  tab_header(title = my_title) %>% 
  fmt_number(
    columns = where(is.numeric),
    n_sigfig = 3,
    use_seps = FALSE
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c("n_patients", "n_obs", "n_event"), drop_trailing_zeros = TRUE
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "cadetblue3"),
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
      ),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = Variables 
    )
  ) %>% 
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
    ),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = p.value,
      rows = p.value < 0.05
    )
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "firebrick"),
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
    ),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = n_event,
      rows = n_event <= 10
    )
  ) %>%
  cols_label_with(
    fn = function(x) gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  ) %>% 
  opt_row_striping()
gt_table

cat("<br>")
cat("\n\n")
```



# Analyse des critères secondaires

----			

**blabla --here**


# Analyse des événements indésirables

----			

**blabla --here**

\newpage

# Ressources

----			

Citer les packages R utilisés pour réaliser l’analyse

**blabla --here**

<br>

•	R Core Team (2021). R: A language and environment for statistical computing. R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria. URL https://www.R-project.org/.

•	Rstudio : Posit team (2024). RStudio: Integrated Development Environment for R. Posit Software, PBC, Boston, MA. URL http://www.posit.co/.

•	Dowle M, Srinivasan A (2023). data.table: Extension of data.frame. R package version 1.14.8, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=data.table.

•	Wickham H, Bryan J (2023). readxl: Read Excel Files. R package version 1.4.2, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=readxl.

•	Wickham H, François R, Henry L, Müller K, Vaughan D (2023). dplyr: A Grammar of Data Manipulation_. R package version 1.1.2, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=dplyr 

•	H. Wickham. ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis. Springer-Verlag New York, 2016.

• (sources à venir)

<!-- --here  -->


•	« R2Excel » https://github.com/GHICL-DRCI/R2Excel.
